chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمَةِ أَنْ تَلْبَسَهُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَ الْحُلِيِّ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ لَهَا مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 85 – What is allowed for the female in Ihraam to wear from the clothes, and the ornaments, and what is disliked for her from that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/288)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/288)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْمَرْأَةُ الْمُحْرِمَةُ تَلْبَسُ مَا شَاءَتْ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ غَيْرَ الْحَرِيرِ وَ الْقُفَّازَيْنِ وَ كُرِهَ النِّقَابُ
وَ قَالَ تَسْدِلُ الثَّوْبَ عَلَى وَجْهِهَا قُلْتُ حَدُّ ذَلِكَ إِلَى أَيْنَ قَالَ إِلَى طَرَفِ الْأَنْفِ قَدْرَ مَا تُبْصِرُ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan, from Ays Bin Al Qasim who said,
‘Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘The woman in Ihraam can wear whatever she so desires to from the clothes, apart from the silk, and the two gloves; but the veil (Naqaab) is disliked’.

And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘And she can hang the cloth upon her face’. I said, ‘The limit of that is up to where?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Up to the side of the nose, to a measurement of what she can see with’.
en.sarwar: 1. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan from ‘Is ibn al-Qasim who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘A woman in the state of Ihram can wear whatever she wants of clothes except silk and al-Quffazayn (something women wear on their hands) and wearing veil is undesirable.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘They must hang something over their face.’ He asked, ‘How far down over the face?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘It should be down over the nose so that she can see without her eyes being blocked.’”
